questions.question: What does VYSE represent and how is it marked on the airspeed indicator?

options.text: Best angle of climb speed, marked with a red line
Best rate of climb speed with one engine inoperative, marked with a blue line
Safe single-engine speed, marked with a green arc
Minimum control speed, marked with a blue line

questions.explanation: VYSE is the best rate-of-climb speed with one engine inoperative. It is marked with a blue radial line on most airspeed indicators.

questions.question: What is VMC and how is it marked on the airspeed indicator?

options.text: Maximum cruise speed, marked with a yellow arc
Minimum control speed with critical engine inoperative, marked with a red radial line
Maneuvering speed, marked with a white arc
Best glide speed, marked with a green arc

questions.explanation: VMC is the minimum control speed with the critical engine inoperative. It is marked with a red radial line on most airspeed indicators.

questions.question: What is VSSE?

options.text: The speed at which the airplane lifts off the surface
The best angle-of-climb speed with one engine inoperative
The safe, intentional one-engine-inoperative speed
The best rate of climb speed with all engines operating

questions.explanation: VSSE is the safe, intentional one-engine-inoperative speed. It is the minimum speed to intentionally render the critical engine inoperative and is required to be published in the AFM/POH by 14 CFR Part 23.

questions.question: If an engine failure occurs below VMC while the airplane is on the ground, what should the pilot do?

options.text: Continue the takeoff and climb at VYSE
Reject the takeoff by closing both throttles and using rudder and brakes
Apply full power on the remaining engine
Raise the landing gear immediately

questions.explanation: If an engine failure occurs below VMC while on the ground, the takeoff must be rejected. Directional control can only be maintained by promptly closing both throttles and using rudder and brakes as required.

questions.question: After liftoff in a multi-engine airplane, what speed should be maintained until reaching a safe single-engine maneuvering altitude?

options.text: VMC
VSSE
VY
VR

questions.explanation: After liftoff, VY should be established and maintained until reaching a safe single-engine maneuvering altitude, typically a minimum of 400-500 feet AGL.
